mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over the trailing three months signals expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns positive over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend effect independent of any threshold crossing.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=134289.0 as of t-1mo, consistent with post-crisis payroll recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive PAYEMS growth months show no statistically significant return premium versus negative-growth months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate, specifically UNRATE increasing more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.0 as of t-1mo, near cycle lows post-2008
failure_condition: In triggered instances, mean forward 5-day SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or hit rate of negative returns among triggered instances falls below 55% over any 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) accelerate while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, the joint condition signals an interest-rate-sensitive housing recovery that feeds through construction employment, consumer wealth effects, and cyclical sector earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. Neither series alone is sufficient; the combination of rising starts and accommodative rates is required.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month growth > 5% AND FEDFUNDS below its trailing 12-month median
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with housing recovery
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 60-day SPX returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns are not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate policy regime, low and stable CPI inflation readings (CPIAUCSL month-over-month change below 0.2%) reinforce market expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain accommodative policy, compressing equity risk premia and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The effect is conditional on the prevailing zero-rate regime and does not generalize to tightening cycles.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is at the effective lower bound, low-CPI months fail to show significantly higher forward 20d SPX returns than high-CPI months (p > 0.05), or the relationship reverses sign outside the zero-rate regime without being flagged as regime failure.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in industrial production growth (INDPRO 3-month annualized growth falling below 1%) signals weakening manufacturing activity and impending earnings downgrades for cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon once the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 60-day SPX returns with hit rate of negative outcomes below 55% over any 3-year window, or mean return not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A retail sales (RSAFS) growth rate crossing above 0.5% month-over-month signals resilient consumer demand, which supports forward earnings estimates for consumer discretionary and broad market indices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS threshold triggers and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or triggered-instance mean return is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of decelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS 3-month average below prior 6-month average) combined with a simultaneous uptick in the unemployment rate (UNRATE rising) constitutes an early labor-market inflection signal analogous to the Sahm rule, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as markets rapidly reprice recession risk. Neither series alone captures the inflection; the combination is required.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 6-month average growth AND UNRATE rising month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to produce significantly negative mean forward 5-day SPX returns (p > 0.05), or hit rate of negative outcomes falls below 55% over any 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery phase, sustained industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals durable economic reacceleration that markets systematically underprice due to lingering recession-era pessimism, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. This effect is specific to early recovery regimes and is expected to weaken or reverse in mature expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=11206.1 as of t-5mo indicating below-trend recovery output level
failure_condition: During identified recovery regimes, INDPRO growth shows Rank IC below 0.03 against forward 60d SPX returns over any 2-year window, or the positive relationship fails to hold with statistical significance (p > 0.05) while a comparable relationship is found in mature-expansion control periods, indicating the regime conditioning is spurious.
